text: The proper function of man is to live, not to exist. I shall not waste my days in trying to prolong them. I shall use my time.
author: Jack London

text: Death is but crossing the world, as friends do the seas; they live in one another still. For they must needs be present, that love and live in that which is omnipresent. In this divine glass, they see face to face; and their converse is free as well as pure. This is the comfort of friends, that though they may be said to die, yet their friendship and society are, in the best sense, ever present, because immortal.
author: William Penn

text: We are closer to God when we are asking questions than when we think we have the answers.
author: Abraham Joshua Heschel

text: Time is free, but it's priceless. You can't own it, but you can use it. You can't keep it, but you can spend it. Once you've lost it you can never get it back.
author: Harvey MacKay

text: A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
author: King Solomon, Proverbs 17:17

text: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.
author: Joshua 1:9

text: A wave can kill you. Or you can ride it. It's sometimes more dangerous to shy away. You can't live your life in fear. You have to be prepared to get on your board and stand on your feet.
author: Matt Haig

text: He who fears he shall suffer, already suffers what he fears.
author: Michel de Montaigne

text: Then He arose and rebuked the wind and said to the sea, "Peace! Be still!" And the wind ceased and there was great calm.
author: Mark 4:39

text: I think we should follow a simple rule: if we can take the worst, take the risk.
author: Dr. Joyce Brothers

text: Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might.
author: Not sure

text: But those who trust in the Lord will find new strength. They will soar high on wings like eagles. They will run and not grow weary. They will walk and not faint.
author: Isaiah 40:31

text: The time will come when, with elation, you will greet yourself arriving at your own door, in your own mirror and each will smile at the other's welcome, and say, sit here. Eat. You will love again the stranger who was your self. Give wine. Give bread, Give back your heart to itself, to the stranger who has loved you all your life, whom you ignored for another, who knows you by heart. Take down the love letters from the bookshelf the photographs, the desperate notes, peel your own image from the mirror. Sit. Feast on your life.
author: Derek Walcott

text: Fix your mind upon its object. Hold to this, unswerving, Disowning fear and hope, Advance only upon this goal.
author: Arjun's divine instruction

text: Presence of mind, and courage in distress, Are more than armies to procure success.
author: John Dryden

text: you can't make homes out of human beings someone should have already told you that and if he wants to leave then let him leave you are terrifying and strange and beautiful something not everyone knows how to love.
author: Warsan Shire
source: For Women Who Are Difficult to Love

text: Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a heaven for?
author: Robert Browning

text: No matter how love-sick a woman is, she shouldn't take the first pill that comes along.
author: Dr. Joyce Brothers

text: Whoever restrains his words has knowledge, and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding.
author: King Solomon, Proverbs 17:27

text: Listening, not imitation, may be the sincerest form of flattery.
author: Dr. Joyce Brothers

text: Knowledge is finite. Wonder is infinite.
author: Matt Haig

text: Yea, and if some god shall wreck me in the wine-dark deep, even so I will endure... For already have I suffered full much, and much have I toiled in perils of waves and war. Let this be added to the tale of those.
author: Homer

text: The world is too much with us.
author: William Wordsworth
source: Sonnet: The World is too much with us

text: Don't aim for perfection. Evolution, and life, only happen through mistakes.
author: Matt Haig

text: For even the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
author: Mark 10:45

text: For the ways are many, but death is one. Wherefore then should you be afraid?
author: Not sure

text: It's not how do I feel about them? it's how do they make me feel?
author: Matthew Hussey

text: The more you give, the more you get. The more you laugh, the less you fret. The more you do unselfishly, the more you live abundantly. The more of everything you share, the more you'll always have to spare. The more you love, the more you'll find that life is good and friends are kind. For only what we give away, enriches us from day to day.
author: Helen Steiner Rice

text: There is no happiness like that of being loved by your fellow creatures, and feeling that your presence is an addition to their comfort.
author: Charlotte Bronte

text: Time heals everything. I don't need anything. Hallelujah.
author: Beyonce

text: Faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.
author: Bible
source: Paul's second letter to the Hebrews

text: When I look at myself in the mirror, I thank God the same old jerk is still staring back at me.
author: Roberto Canessa

text: Our idea of God tells us more about ourselves than about Him.
author: Thomas Merton

text: You create your own luck.
Fake it till you make it.
Life begins outside of your comfort zone.
When we stop learning and growing we stop living.
Listen with the same passion of wanting to be heard.
If someone is trying to get you down that just means you're above them.
Pay attention to who you are with when you feel your best.
Choose discomfort over resentment.
Stop comparing your insides to other people's outsides.
Handsome is as handsome does.
Love the thing that loves you back.
Comparison is the thief of joy.
Sometimes we choose our illnesses since they may benefit us.
Live a life of oh wells instead of what ifs.
If we only wanted to be happy, it would be easy; but we want to be happier than other people, which is almost always difficult, since we think them happier than they are.
author: Charles de Montesquieu

text: Everything you see or hear or experience in any way at all is specific to you. You create a universe by perceiving it, so everything in the universe you perceive is specific to you.
Respect yourself enough to walk away from anything that no longer serves you, grows you, or makes you happy.
author: Robert Tew

text: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.
author: Dumbledore

text: People like to feel important. Make them feel important and they'll appreciate you.
A healthy man wants a thousand things a sick man only wants one.
author: Confucius

text: Life is what happens when you're busy making plans.
Only way out is through.
The popular belief isn't always the correct one.
You're your problem, but you're also your solution.
The worst decision is indecision.
I fear not the man who has practiced 10,000 kicks once, but I fear the man who has practiced one kick 10,000 times.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Hard work will open every door in front of you. But you will not be in front of every door.
Go further, go farther, if not why bother?
author: Jay Z

text: Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will.
Everyone has a plan until they get punched in the face.
You teach people how to treat you.
You cannot consider the measure of something without considering the measurer first.
Face what you're afraid of and you'll be free.
author: Jordan Peterson

text: Bravery is better than safety.
author: Jordan Peterson

text: If you are surrounded by people who are dull, try listening more.
author: Jordan Peterson

text: Things you regret the most are things you value the most.
When in doubt always reach out.
The way we describe our experience becomes the memory.
The harder you work the luckier you get.
A bend in the road is not the end of the road.
You can walk through life much easier with a smile on your face.
Luck is the residue of design.
author: Branch Ricky

text: O God and Heavenly Father, Grant to us the serenity of mind to accept that which cannot be changed; courage to change that which can be changed, and wisdom to know the one from the other, through Jesus Christ our Lord, Amen.
author: Serenity Prayer

text: All we have to do is decide what to do with the time that is given to us.
author: Gandalf

text: Anything you can put your hands on in this life you cannot take with you - be proud of the work you put in.
Luxuries can often times become necessities.
End? No, the journey doesn't end here. Death is just another path, one that we all must take. The grey rain-curtain of this world rolls back, and all turns to silver glass, and then you see it.
author: Gandalf

text: It is difficult to get a man to understand something when his salary depends on his not understanding it.
author: Upton Sinclair

text: A good leader takes a little more than his share of the blame, a little less than his share of the credit.
You are not a failure until you start blaming others for your mistakes.
author: John Wooden

text: Biology enables, culture forbids.
Always maintain a healthy skepticism for anyone claiming to be authority, at least till they prove themselves capable.
Compliment choices not defaults.
Think about how people are receiving the information you're saying.
But in the end, it's only a passing thing, this shadow. Even darkness must pass. A new day will come. And when the sun shines it will shine out the clearer.
author: Samwise Gamgee

text: The key to a successful leader is to earn respect for character, not rank.
author: Dick Winters

text: Do you want to walk around all day knowing that you could but you didn't?
author: David Goggins

text: No assumption until you see the evidence.
There's only one standard. Your own.
There's a difference between fear and losing your nerve.
When making decisions, would my future self hope for that choice?
I know that I don't know.
author: Socrates

text: A spouse is not a destination but a fellow traveler.
Luck is when preparation and opportunity meet.
The devil you know is better than the devil you don't.
Everybody is a hero in their own story.
I try very hard not to take things personal that I don't think are meant to be personal.
author: Tim Cook

text: Real love isn't said, it's felt.
If you can't make a decision, go with your first choice. If you still don't know, the answer is no.
author: RJS

text: It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
author: Dumbledore

text: Without rain nothing grows, learn to embrace the storms in your life.
Be loyal to what matters.
author: Arthur Morgan

text: Work like you're broke.
Every new invention has practice use, accidental harm, and premeditated harm.
Work while others are wishing.
Nothing ventured nothing gained.
May you live in interesting times.

Conserve your forces and energies by keeping them concentrated at their strongest point. You gain more by finding a rich mine and mining it deeper, than by flitting from one shallow mine to another — intensity defeats extensity every time.
If you play silly games you will win silly prizes.
Silence breeds whispers.
In business output matters more than input.
Take the word fair out of your vocabulary.
It is possible to commit no mistakes and still lose. That is not a weakness. That is life.
Pain demands to be felt.
The courage to be oneself.
Opportunity is not a lengthy visitor.
author: Stephen Sondheim

text: There is no time like the present.
If I'm not for myself, who will be? If I'm only for myself, what am I?
Knowledge isn't power until it is applied.
The opposite of love is not hate, it's indifference.
author: Elie Wiesel

text: The very wind that seeks to blow out a fire may cause it to spread.
It's hard to hate up close.
author: Michelle Obama

text: Make the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil.
author: Ephesians 5:16

text: The phrase a bird in the hand is worth two in the bush is used for saying that it's better to hold onto something one has already than to risk losing it by trying to attain something better.
You can lead a horse to water but you can't make it drink.
27 percent of a watermelon is a whole lot better than 27 percent of a grape.
author: Mark Cuban

text: Wise men make more opportunities than they find.
Happiness is a choice that requires effort at times.
author: Aeschylus

text: Don't fear nothin but don't be no fool.
The learn-it-all will always do better than the know-it-all.
We judge others by their actions, we judge ourselves by our intentions.
Hero worship might make Americans forget that laws mattered more than men.
author: Calvin Coolidge

text: Don't take chances, take calculated risks.
Winning is easy. It's the struggle that defines the man.
author: Ron Howard

text: Words are wind.
author: George R.R. Martin

text: Do not wait for chance.
Lust looks like love until it's time to sacrifice.
Fools talk, cowards are silent, wise men listen.
It is our choices that show who we are far greater than our ability.
First thing you need to ask yourself about criticism is: is it fair?
author: Dr J

text: Begin with the end in mind.
What would you attempt to do if you knew you could not fail?
Let your energy impact the people you're talking to, as opposed to the other way around.
Battles are won months before they're fought.
Plan like a pessimist and dream like an optimist.
The secret of happiness is to always have something to look forward to.

Nothing in the world can take the place of persistence. Talent will not; nothing is more common than unsuccessful men with talent. Genius will not; unrewarded genius is almost a proverb. Education will not; the world is full of educated derelicts.
author: Calvin Coolidge

text: Nobody knows what they're doing before they do it. Growth happens outside of your comfort zone.
Creativity is an active process, not a passive one.
Start with what you want to achieve, instead of limiting yourself to what's realistic or sustainable.
Don't run away from what you don't want; run toward what you do.
Your new life will cost you your old one.
In self judgement: It's your second thought and your first action that you're responsible for. Your first thought, throw it away.
Star in your role.
There's no great loss without some gain.
It's hard to grow if you don't challenge yourself.
It takes all types of people to make the world go around.
There's a lid for every pot.
Well done is better than well said.
When you're in the monkey house for too long you stop smelling the shit.
Not every challenge is trauma.
Sometimes life gives you what you need not what you want.
I'm going to support you in your failure as much as your success.
The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
author: Warren Buffett

text: To know thyself is the beginning of wisdom.
author: Socrates

text: Constant vigilance.
Where you go I will go, and where you stay I will stay. Your people will be my people and your God my God.
Business is a matter of human service.
author: MS Hershey

text: Know your way out before you go in.
Real Gs move in silence like lasagna.
It is not the answers you bring back that matter, it is the person that you become while seeking them.
Thriving is surviving.
Never tell people how to do things. Tell them what to do and they will surprise you with their ingenuity.
You only live once, but if you do it right, once is enough.

If your body was turned over to just anyone, you would doubtless take exception. Why aren't you ashamed that you have made your mind vulnerable to anyone who happens to criticize you, so that it automatically becomes confused and upset?
author: Epictetus

text: Don't waste the rest of your time here worrying about other people unless it affects the common good. It will keep you from doing anything useful.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You have to laugh so you don't weep.
Great minds discuss ideas; average minds discuss events; small minds discuss people.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: I consider my ability to arouse enthusiasm among my people the greatest asset I possess, and the way to develop the best that is in a person is by appreciation and encouragement.
author: Charles Schwab

text: He that has once done you a kindness will be more ready to do you another, than he whom you yourself have obliged.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: I shall pass this way but once; any good that I can do or any kindness I can show to any human being; let me do it now. Let me not defer nor neglect it, for I shall not pass this way again.
author: Etienne de Grellet

text: Everyone likes a compliment.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: You can make more friends in two months by becoming interested in other people than you can in two years by trying to get other people interested in you.
author: Dale Carnegie

text: The expression one wears on one's face is far more important than the clothes one wears on one's back.
author: Dale Carnegie

text: Belief is not a matter of choice, but conviction.
author: Unknown

text: A plan is only as good as those who see it through.
The best confidence builder is experience.
Ignore your instincts at your peril.
Fail with honor rather than succeed by fraud.
It is a rough road that leads to the heights of greatness.
Sometimes, accepting help is harder than offering it.
Who's the more foolish, the fool or the fool who follows him?
Humility is the only defense against humiliation.
It's hard to be hungry when you have.
author: Michael Jordan

text: You can catch more flies with a drop of honey than a bucket of gall.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: I have no right to say or do anything that diminishes a man in his own eyes. What matters is not what I think of him, but what he thinks of himself. Hurting a man in his dignity is a crime.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

text: Man is condemned to be free; because once thrown into the world, he is responsible for everything he does. It is up to you to give life a meaning.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world; indeed, it's the only thing that ever has.
author: Margaret Mead

text: May the worst days of your future be better than the best days of your past.
author: David Leadbeater

text: The labyrinth is a place of transformation. When you walk the labyrinth, you are not lost - you are finding your way. At the center lies wisdom, at the entrance lies courage, and on the path lies truth. The Minotaur is not a monster to be slain, but a part of yourself to be understood.
author: Ancient Labyrinth Wisdom
